prompt:
RE-RENDER cortex through the TASTE SPEC (this is the measurable test that the render path now consumes David's rated taste). READ FIRST, in order: (1) docs/david-design-patterns.md — David's rated design taste, distilled from 156 designs; (2) the love-tier exemplar SHOTS to anchor on: tools/mocha-census/out/shots/ad-apps-angeleye__wave-bar.png, ad-apps-angeleye__wave-subagents.png, ad-apps-dark-factory__06-blackboard-vs-poem.png, ad-apps-dark-factory__07-workflow-flows.png (these are what LOVE looks like — drawn diagrams, legends, I/O headers, semantic block colour); (3) .claude/skills/comprehend-visualise/SKILL.md §'Render conditioning'; (4) appydave-plugins/appydave/skills/mochaccino/references/usage-patterns.md.

The comprehension DATA already exists at experiments/watchtower-engine/proof/cortex/data.json (cortex @ a129a5d50ba8a140c288b6f2eb03ddadcee873a9). Audience = David's YouTube viewers asking 'HOW DOES A BRAIN ACTUALLY WORK?'. DATA-FIRST/MVC: index.html fetches its data/NN-*.json, never inlines.

This re-render must FIX the three rated failures the previous version (06/02/03/04 currently live) committed:
  (A) NO DIAGRAM → the #1 love-trigger is a DRAWN DIAGRAM (svg: love 3.5 vs avg 0.3). The previous version was card-grids + numbered rails = caps at 'good', the card-grid is 'average'. REDRAW as actual SVG diagrams: curvy/clean connectors (THIN, NEUTRAL — colour lives in the BLOCKS not the lines), a LEGEND strip, and an INPUT/OUTPUT header.
  (B) ORANGE/YELLOW-ON-BROWN/BEIGE → David's #1 documented failure (13 gripes, docks even loved designs). DROP the yellow #ffde59 / gold-on-cream scheme. Block colour = the GREEN/BLUE/PURPLE semantic set (one hue per memory region); orange/yellow ONLY as a tiny highlight, NEVER text-on-brown or filled boxes on beige. Keep light-mode primary; dark surfaces are accents only.
  (C) DENSE PROSE + UNIFORM CHROME → ease density: ONE short blurb per element (labels over paragraphs), trim the multi-field prose (no stacked blurb+under_the_hood+thesis+example walls). DO NOT add the why/who/what-question Orient strip (it is DEMOTED — not a love-trigger; it flattened all 4 into the same shape). Add GHOST TEXT (oversized tone-on-tone background lettering — David's signature favourite) and reward asymmetry/uniqueness per design.

DELIVER FOUR designs in the EXISTING .mochaccino/ workspace, redrawn as a coherent DIAGRAM set (server already serving :7422 — regenerate files, do NOT start a new port; curl to verify):
  06-brain-anatomy (HERO) — the brain as ONE labelled SVG diagram: ingest = an INPUT arrow into the brain (I/O header), the FOUR memory regions as colour-coded lobes/zones (Episodic/Semantic/Associative/Housekeeping, one semantic hue each), recall = a flow lighting the regions = OUTPUT, and the THREE SWAPPABLE ORGANS (Storage/Embedding/LLM) drawn as PLUGGABLE SOCKETS connected to the kernel — SHOW 'swap an organ, keep the mind', do not just write it. Legend strip. Ghost 'BRAIN' behind.
  02-brain-remembers — the remember→recall FLOW as a diagram (conversation in → extract who/what → timeline → meaning fingerprint → recall blends the senses), connectors + legend + I/O header.
  03-four-memories — the four regions as a colour-coded DIAGRAM (NOT a flat card-grid), each zone its semantic hue + its cortex db under-the-hood as a small tag.
  04-brain-sleeps — the sleep cycle as a circular/flow DIAGRAM (decay → tag → merge → link → tier), connectors + legend.

Reframe the data/*.json as needed (trim prose, add diagram fields); KEEP provenance meta.source = cortex path + commit_sha. Regenerate designs/index.html gallery: cortex cluster grouped, hero 06 first; keep 01-session-journey + 05-dark-factory. Verify all four + gallery over http on :7422 with curl.

Report ONE line: gallery URL + the 4 design names + which of the 3 failures (A/B/C) each design now fixes. Then complete run-next-workflow bookkeeping (run record + move to done/ + reports/ handback — DO NOT skip the reports/ handback).

args.label: cortex-brain-v3
args.recipe: diagram-first + semantic-block-colour + kill-yellow-on-brown + ghost-text + less-prose + no-orient-strip
